name: Ray On Slurm
summary: Launch resource-bounded Ray clusters inside Slurm allocations.
description: This skill helps users start Ray across Slurm-allocated nodes while keeping head-node selection, the head port, CPU/GPU resources, temporary directories, logs, and smoke-test execution explicit.

inputs.name: python_script
inputs.description: Python script to run on the Ray head node after the cluster starts.

inputs.name: ray_port_policy
inputs.description: Optional site policy for the head port and, when using a manual ray start pattern, non-overlapping Ray service and worker ports.

inputs.name: python_environment
inputs.description: Optional virtualenv or Conda/Mamba environment containing ray.

inputs.name: resources
inputs.description: Slurm nodes, tasks, CPUs, GPUs, memory, and wall time requested for the Ray smoke test.

outputs.name: slurm_log
outputs.description: Slurm stdout/stderr log for the Ray job.

outputs.name: ray_record
outputs.description: Launch plan, selected head node, address, Ray version, optional port policy, and smoke-test status.

outputs.name: ray_result
outputs.description: Small JSON result report from the Ray demonstration workload.

examples.title: Plan or launch a Ray cluster in Slurm

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Check the Slurm wrapper shell syntax.

Replace account, partition, and environment placeholders, submit with RUN_RAY_SMOKE=0 to review the launch plan, then submit with RUN_RAY_SMOKE=1 on a short test allocation and confirm the result JSON is written.
